compiled_state.title: As a developer, diffs with external API calls or unfamiliar imports get standard-tier review minimum
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a classifier floor rule that bumps light‚Üístandard when the diff introduces external API calls or imports not in the project dependency manifest.
**Why**: Hallucination detection and fragile-vs-nitpick assessment require at minimum a sonnet-class model. Haiku cannot reliably make these distinctions.
**Scope**:
- IN: New _has_external_api_signal() floor rule in review-complexity-classifier.sh, dependency manifest discovery and resolution, fail-open behavior for absent manifests
- OUT: Hallucination verification instructions (Story 2). ESCALATE_REVIEW (Story 3).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, diffs containing import statements for packages not listed in the project dependency manifest (pyproject.toml, package.json, or requirements.txt) are classified at standard tier minimum
  ‚Üê Satisfies: "The classifier bumps light‚Üístandard when the diff introduces external API calls or imports not already in the project's dependency manifest"
- When this story is complete, the classifier discovers the dependency manifest by searching for pyproject.toml, package.json, or requirements.txt relative to REPO_ROOT, and fails open (no bump) when no manifest is found
  ‚Üê Satisfies: "The classifier bumps light‚Üístandard" (graceful degradation)
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ‚Üê Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Adversarial] Manifest discovery must handle monorepo layouts (multiple pyproject.toml), subdirectory manifests (app/), and alternative formats (requirements.txt). Define resolution order and fail-open behavior
- [Reliability] The classifier currently only reads diff from stdin and uses git/filesystem patterns ‚Äî adding manifest file reads introduces a new class of I/O that must not cause classifier failure (exit non-zero ‚Üí default to standard, masking the floor rule)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue ‚Äî one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
